translation: And Salmon begot Boaz by Rahab; and Boaz begot Obed by Ruth; and Obed begot Jesse.

alternate_translations.translation: Salmon fathered Boaz through Rahab; Boaz fathered Obed through Ruth; Obed fathered Jesse.
alternate_translations.notes: “ἐγέννησεν” can be rendered as “fathered/brought forth” (not just “begot”). “ἐκ τῆς/της” here indicates the mother (“by/through”), so “through Rahab/Ruth” is a valid smoothing while keeping the sense.

alternate_translations.translation: Salmon begat Boaz from Rahab; Boaz begat Obed from Ruth; Obed begat Jesse.
alternate_translations.notes: “ἐκ” literally means “from,” and in this genealogical formula it can be taken as “from” (i.e., through/with the woman named). This is a more literal rendering.

notes: This is a standard biblical genealogical formula in Greek: “A begot B from/through C.” The names correspond to figures in the Matthew/Old Testament lineage tradition (Rahab, Ruth, Jesse). The English “begot” is archaic but conveys the original meaning precisely.
